title: Permissions to give contractors you want to have editing capabilities
excerpt: We are a small company working on a video about a project. We are hiring a few contractors to do the editing and animations. I would love to use Monday.com to manage the project so that we end up with all the clips of th…

posts.author: Emily_Lundberg (Emily E. Lundberg)
posts.cookedHtml: We are a small company working on a video about a project. We are hiring a few contractors to do the editing and animations. I would love to use Monday.com to manage the project so that we end up with all the clips of the footage well labeled and easy to use and find, given that is the bulk of the work that the contractors will be doing before they put the video together. I am not sure I understand how best to manage this. It will entail the contractors needing permission to do all things on this board – manipulate everything. I can’t remember exactly how billing works with new members vs. new guests vs. I don’t know. What is the best and most economical way to set up their membership/permissions? Thank you.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Emily!

If you are on a Pro plan, you can add unlimited number of Guest users for free!

This would be the best way to add contractors to your account so that they can do everything without being able to change your account settings or see billing info.

You will want to make sure the boards they’re working with are Shareable Boards because Guests can only use Shareable Boards but they have all the same abilities within those boards as a regular Member on your account.

Does this help? Feel free to send us a DM on Twitter @mondaysupport if you want to chat further!

posts.author: JDer (Daniel Jackman)
posts.cookedHtml: I worked with freelancers for a few of our project videos, and permissions were honestly the trickiest part to figure out. We ended up setting them up as Guests in Monday.com so they could handle most of the editing workflow without messing with our admin stuff. Just made sure everything was on Shareable Boards.

posts.author: JDer (Daniel Jackman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I had to double-check where that line is between guest privileges and the limitations, but once it’s in place, it works smoothly without adding to the billing.

As for making that editing step easier, I’ve been testing different tools and found some useful comparisons for best AVI video editors: https://www.movavi.com/learning-portal/best-avi-video-editors.html. Helped me figure out what to recommend to our contractors based on their setup.
